summary: A legacy-to-new system migration is a governed discipline, not an ad-hoc load: every new element declares its origin, the change set is reconciled as-built, and each apply is verified (positive and negative) and recorded.

rules.rule: Every new schema element introduced for the new system declares its origin class (DERIVED with its formula/backfill, GREENFIELD, or SEED) — no new schema element is admitted without one.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Add the MIGRATION-PLAYBOOK row (what changed, origin class, formula/backfill, cutover gotchas) in the same change-set/PR that makes the schema change — as-built, never reconstructed from memory.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Every migration follows written-not-applied → verify → apply, where verification covers both the positive check (the change does what it should) and the negative check (no unintended drop, orphaned data, or constraint rejecting valid legacy rows).

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Never apply a migration out of the explicitly declared dependency order — dependencies are declared, not assumed, and the playbook orders the batch.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Never store apply credentials in the repo and never use the application's runtime credential for a migration — hydrate an admin/owner credential at apply time.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Apply each migration surgically in a transaction with a self-test (failed self-test rolls back) and leave an apply-record of what migration, when, who (real attributable identity), and result.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate
